image:image.image:title: Cassia Co-op — Mount Kerinci, Sumatra
image:image.image:caption: Origin of Cassia Co-op cinnamon — Kerinci Highlands, Sumatra, Indonesia

image:image.image:title: From farmer to end user — Cassia Co-op origin map
image:image.image:caption: Single origin, fully traceable, certified organic Korintje cinnamon (Cinnamomum burmannii) from Sumatra

image:image.image:title: Korintje cassia trees — Kerinci Highlands
image:image.image:caption: Cinnamomum burmannii single origin cinnamon from Sumatra

image:image.image:title: Cassia plantation under rainforest canopy
image:image.image:caption: Rainforest Alliance certified cinnamon plantation, Sumatra

image:image.image:title: Cassia seedling nursery — Kerinci
image:image.image:caption: Cassia Co-op replanting programme for partner farmers

image:image.image:title: Cassia bark drying — Kerinci facility
image:image.image:caption: Sun-curing cassia bark before grinding, FSSC 22000 facility

image:image.image:title: Cassia bark crushing — Kerinci facility
image:image.image:caption: FSSC 22000 certified processing of cassia in Sumatra

image:image.image:title: Cassia processing machine — Kerinci facility
image:image.image:caption: Cassia Co-op grinding equipment, FSSC 22000 certified

image:image.image:title: Premium cassia sticks — Korintje cinnamon
image:image.image:caption: Premium-grade cassia cinnamon sticks from Sumatra
